problem_statement: K-12 students and their parents face a painful gap between expensive human tutoring ($50-150/hr) and AI tutoring tools that are inaccurate (50% math accuracy for ChatGPT), fail to genuinely teach (students score 17% worse on tests despite solving more practice problems), and cannot replace the personalized attention of human tutors.
target_user: K-12 students (ages 5-18) and their parents, particularly middle-income families who cannot afford premium human tutors ($50-150/hr) but need personalized academic support across subjects
evidence_summary: 30+ unique complaint threads found across Reddit, Trustpilot, EdWeek, Hechinger Report, LinkedIn, Quora, NBC News, CNN, and education blogs. Pattern: Two intersecting pain points — (1) human tutoring is prohibitively expensive at $50-150/hr with families spending $2800/mo, and (2) existing AI alternatives have fundamental accuracy and pedagogical flaws (ChatGPT 50% math accuracy, UPenn study showing 17% worse test scores). Parents describe being 'desperate' and tutoring being 'impossible for most families'. 70% of teachers worry AI weakens critical thinking. 89% of students use ChatGPT but most cannot use it effectively.
